title: Digital Twin Cost Comparator
description: Industrial-grade cost comparison tool for digital twin implementations, incorporating ISO 55000, Lean, Six Sigma, and WERC standards.

inputs.label: Digital Twin Scope
inputs.businessContext: Select the scope of the digital twin: asset, process, system, or enterprise. Influences complexity and cost multipliers.

inputs.label: Number of Assets/Entities
inputs.businessContext: Total number of physical assets or entities to be modeled in the digital twin.

inputs.label: Sensor Density (sensors per asset)
inputs.businessContext: Average number of IoT sensors per asset. Higher density increases data acquisition and processing costs.

inputs.label: Data Acquisition Frequency
inputs.businessContext: Frequency of data collection: 0.1 (every 10s), 1 (per second), 10 (10 Hz), 100 (100 Hz). Higher frequency increases data volume and cost.

inputs.label: Integration Complexity
inputs.businessContext: Complexity of integrating with existing systems (ERP, MES, CMMS). Low, medium, high. Affects labor and software costs.

inputs.label: Data Quality Index (DQI)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of data that is accurate, complete, and timely. Based on ISO 8000 data quality standards.

inputs.label: Average Labor Rate (USD/hour)
inputs.businessContext: Blended hourly rate for engineering and IT staff involved in digital twin development and maintenance.

inputs.label: Expected Lifespan (years)
inputs.businessContext: Expected operational lifespan of the digital twin system. Used for amortization and total cost of ownership calculations.

inputs.label: Lean Six Sigma Maturity Level
inputs.businessContext: Organizational maturity in Lean Six Sigma: level1 (initial), level2 (managed), level3 (defined), level4 (quantitatively managed), level5 (optimizing). Affects efficiency and waste reduction.

inputs.label: WERC DC Benchmark (cost per order)
inputs.businessContext: Warehouse Education and Research Council (WERC) benchmark for average cost per order in distribution centers.

rules.message: For asset-level twin, asset count should not exceed 10,000 without enterprise-level scaling.
Combined sensor density and frequency may exceed typical data pipeline capacity. Consider reducing or using edge processing.
Data quality index below 60% may lead to unreliable digital twin outputs. Improve data governance per ISO 8000.
Short lifespan with high asset count may not yield positive ROI. Consider phased implementation.

thresholds.alert: Cost per asset exceeds $5,000. Investigate scope or integration complexity.
Negative net present value. Reassess business case or reduce scope.
Data confidence adjusted score below 0.7. Improve data quality or increase sensor density.

formulas.name: Annual Data Volume (TB)
formulas.description: Total data volume generated per year based on asset count, sensor density, and data frequency.

formulas.name: Infrastructure Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Cost of cloud/edge infrastructure, storage, and compute based on data volume and integration complexity.

formulas.name: Labor Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Total labor cost over lifespan, adjusted by Lean Six Sigma maturity level.
formulas.expression: asset_count * 10 * labor_rate * expected_lifespan * (1 - 0.05 * (lean_six_sigma_level == 'level5' ? 4 : lean_six_sigma_level == 'level4' ? 3 : lean_six_sigma_level == 'level3' ? 2 : lean_six_sigma_level == 'level2' ? 1 : 0))

formulas.name: Software License Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Annual software licensing cost based on scope and asset count.

formulas.name: Maintenance and Support Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Annual maintenance cost as percentage of infrastructure and software costs.

formulas.name: Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)
formulas.description: Sum of all direct and indirect costs over the lifespan.

formulas.name: Net Present Value (NPV)
formulas.description: NPV of digital twin investment using WERC benchmark savings and discount rate of 8%.

primary.label: Total Cost per Asset (USD)
primary.description: Primary metric: total cost of ownership divided by number of assets.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Data Quality Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Cost impact due to poor data quality (DQI < 100%).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Integration Inefficiency Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Additional cost due to high integration complexity.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Lean Waste Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Cost of non-value-added activities due to low Lean Six Sigma maturity.

suggestedActions.description: Implement data governance framework per ISO 8000 to improve DQI above 85%.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduces data quality loss by up to 40%.

suggestedActions.description: Adopt standardized APIs and middleware to lower integration complexity from high to medium.
Invest in Lean Six Sigma training to move from level 3 to level 4 maturity.
Deploy edge computing to reduce data volume and cloud costs.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Score
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Composite confidence score based on data quality, sensor density, and integration complexity.
